hooks.command: file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|json|md)$'; then npx prettier --write "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$'; then npx eslint --fix "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE 'CURRENT_PHASE$'; then echo '🔄 PHASE BOUNDARY DETECTED — Run these before starting the next phase:'; echo '  1. Spawn auditor agent — full product audit (dead endpoints, schema-UI gaps, pattern inconsistencies)'; echo '  2. Spawn product-critic agent — product quality check (flows, UX, spec fidelity)'; echo '  3. Spawn refactorer agent — codebase cleanup (dedup, naming, dead code)'; echo '  4. Run /audit for security and code quality review'; echo 'Complete all four before proceeding to next phase tickets.'; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if [ -z "$file_path" ] || ! echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$'; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.(test|spec)\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$'; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '(config|setup|\.d\.ts)'; then exit 0; fi; dir=$(dirname "$file_path"); base=$(basename "$file_path"); name="${base%.*}"; ext="${base##*.}"; test_file=''; for candidate in "$dir/$name.test.$ext" "$dir/$name.spec.$ext" "$dir/__tests__/$name.test.$ext" "$dir/__tests__/$name.spec.$ext"; do if [ -f "$candidate" ]; then test_file="$candidate"; break; fi; done; if [ -z "$test_file" ]; then echo "⚠️  No test file found for $file_path. Add a test at $dir/$name.test.$ext"; exit 0; fi; echo "🧪 Running test: $test_file"; result=$(bunx vitest run "$test_file" 2>&1); status=$?; if [ $status -ne 0 ]; then echo "❌ Test failed: $test_file"; echo "$result" | tail -30; exit 1; else echo "✅ Test passed: $test_file"; fi
cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 || true; fi
cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then bun test --passWithNoTests 2>&1 | tail -20 || true; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(auth|api|action|middleware|webhook)'; then echo '⚠️  SECURITY-SENSITIVE FILES IN COMMIT - verify: auth checks, input validation, no secrets'; fi; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then changed=$(git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-only -r HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | grep -vE '\.(test|spec)\.' | head -20); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo '🔍 POST-COMMIT: Run /simplify to review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency:'; echo "$changed"; fi; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit.*\[P[0-9]+-T[0-9]+\]'; then changed=$(git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-only -r HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -vE '\.(test|spec)\.' | head -10); has_test_only=true; for f in $changed; do echo "$f" | grep -qE '\.(test|spec)\.' || { has_test_only=false; break; }; done; if [ "$has_test_only" = false ]; then echo '📝 POST-COMMIT: Spawn a doc-writer agent (haiku model) to update docs/ for the changes in this ticket commit.'; fi; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^(bun add|npm install)'; then echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M') - Dependency installed: $cmd" >> progress/build-log.md 2>/dev/null || true; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^(bun add|npm install)'; then echo '🔒 Running security audit...' && (bun audit 2>/dev/null || npm audit --audit-level=high 2>/dev/null || echo 'Audit not available'); fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^(bun add|npm install)'; then if [ -f package.json ]; then dep_count=$(jq '[.dependencies // {} | length, .devDependencies // {} | length] | add' package.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$dep_count" -gt 40 ]; then echo "⚠️  DEPENDENCY BUDGET: $dep_count total dependencies. Every dependency is attack surface. Review if all are necessary."; elif [ "$dep_count" -gt 30 ]; then echo "📦 Dependency count: $dep_count (approaching budget of 40)"; fi; fi; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then if [ -d src ]; then src_files=$(find src -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.js' -o -name '*.jsx' 2>/dev/null | grep -vcE '\.(test|spec)\.' || echo 0); src_loc=$(find src -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.js' -o -name '*.jsx' 2>/dev/null | grep -vE '\.(test|spec)\.' | xargs cat 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '); echo "📊 Codebase: $src_files source files, $src_loc LOC (excluding tests)"; fi; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '(\.env|\.git/|package-lock\.json|bun\.lockb)'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: Cannot edit protected file: '$file_path >&2 && exit 1; fi; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git commit'; then if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then gitleaks protect --staged --no-banner 2>&1 || (echo '❌ BLOCKED: gitleaks detected secrets in staged changes.' >&2 && exit 1); else if git diff --cached | grep -qiE '(api[_-]?key|secret[_-]?key|password|token|credential|private[_-]?key|client[_-]?secret|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token|bearer|jwt[_-]?secret|signing[_-]?key|encryption[_-]?key|database[_-]?url.*@|mongodb\+srv://|postgres://|mysql://)\s*[=:]'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: Potential secret detected in commit. Remove hardcoded credentials.' >&2 && exit 1; fi; fi; fi; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git push.*(prod|origin.*prod)'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: Cannot push to prod branch. Production deploys are human-only.' >&2 && exit 1; fi; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git (merge.*prod|checkout prod)'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: Cannot merge to or checkout prod branch. Production is human-only.' >&2 && exit 1; fi; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if [ -f bun.lockb ] && echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^npm (install|i|add|ci)'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: Use bun instead of npm in this project' >&2 && exit 1; fi; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '^git push'; then echo '🔍 Running pre-push checks...' && npm run lint && npm run build || (echo '❌ BLOCKED: Fix lint/build errors before pushing' >&2 && exit 1); fi; exit 0
echo '🧪 Running tests before PR creation...' && bun test --passWithNoTests || (echo '❌ BLOCKED: Tests must pass before creating PR' >&2 && exit 1)
afplay -v 0.5 /System/Library/Sounds/Funk.aiff &
afplay -v 0.5 /System/Library/Sounds/Glass.aiff &

if [ -d specs/decisions ]; then pending=$(grep -rl 'Status.*PENDING' specs/decisions/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '); if [ "$pending" -gt 0 ]; then echo "⏳ $pending PENDING spec decision(s) need human input:"; grep -rl 'Status.*PENDING' specs/decisions/ 2>/dev/null | while read f; do name=$(head -1 "$f" | sed 's/^# //'); echo "  - $name ($f)"; done; fi; fi; if [ -d docs/decisions ]; then arch_pending=$(grep -rl 'Status.*PENDING' docs/decisions/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '); if [ "$arch_pending" -gt 0 ]; then echo "⏳ $arch_pending PENDING arch decision(s) need human input:"; grep -rl 'Status.*PENDING' docs/decisions/ 2>/dev/null | while read f; do name=$(head -1 "$f" | sed 's/^# //'); echo "  - $name ($f)"; done; fi; fi

customInstructions:
You are building a CAIO incubator project. Follow the conventions in CLAUDE.md. When running /work, execute tickets continuously — do NOT stop between tickets. After completing and committing a ticket, immediately pick up the next TODO ticket. Only stop when: all tickets are DONE, all remaining tickets are BLOCKED, or a human interrupts. Use TDD. Never merge to prod. Surface decisions via /decision command. Commit after each completed ticket. For large features (L/XL), spawn a feature subagent in a worktree.

SIMPLICITY MANDATE: Prefer the smallest correct implementation. Before adding a function, check if an existing one already does it. Before adding a file, check if it fits in an existing one. Before adding a dependency, check if the standard library or existing deps cover it. Three lines of inline code is better than a premature abstraction. Every function must be under 40 lines, every file under 400 lines, nesting under 3 levels. If you find yourself writing a utility, search the codebase first — it probably already exists. Complexity is a bug.
